widgets_values: If strength is too high, image flexibility decreases and the result may become rigid.

## Custom Nodes Used

- comfyui-ppm
- rgthree-comfy
- ComfyUI_ADV_CLIP_emb
- cg-use-everywhere
- ComfyUI-Advanced-ControlNet

nodes.title: Yumil MPM Note
widgets_values:
## Requirements
- Extension: comfyui-yumil-mpm
- Application: Yumil MPM
- Reference image
---
## Before You Start

- If this is your first time using Yumil MPM, it is a good idea to create a sample project from the app and read at least the introductory tutorial first.
---
## Setup Steps in Yumil MPM

1. In the CategoryTree, open the "PositivePrompt" category.
2. In the PromptTree, create a group named "ControlNet".
3. Add a new prompt named "base" inside the ControlNet group, then paste the template on the right.
4. In the CategoryTree, open the "Pose" category.
5. In the PromptTree for the Pose category, create a group named "ControlNet" at the root level.
6. Add a child prompt to the ControlNet group. Go to PromptTree > Selected:Add > Add From WD14.
7. In the dialog, add the reference image, check the "ComfyUI MPM Parser" checkbox, and click OK.
8. Check the created prompt and run generation.

---
## Notes on Prompt Creation

- When using WD14, Exclude Tags can help remove everything except poses and angles.
- To create output in natural language, Add From LM Studio can be used instead of WD14.
